label: Additionality adjustment default rates

standard.label: Standard (central estimates based on Additionality Guide ranges)
standard.note: Central estimates for a broad range of interventions. The Additionality Guide provides ranges by intervention type, not a single set of defaults. Adjust based on the specific context.

formula: net_additional = gross * (1 - deadweight) * (1 - displacement) * (1 - leakage) * (1 - substitution) * multiplier

multiplier_guidance.description: After computing net additional outputs, apply a multiplier to capture indirect and induced effects. The Additionality Guide 4th Edition explicitly includes this as the final step.

type_1.label: Type I (indirect/supply chain only)
type_1.note: Captures supply chain effects only. Use for most programme evaluations.

type_2.label: Type II (indirect + induced/income)
type_2.note: Captures supply chain effects plus household spending effects. Higher but more uncertain. Use with caution.

multiplier_guidance.note: Default multiplier of 1.0 (no multiplier effect) is conservative. Apply a multiplier > 1.0 only when you have evidence of supply chain or induced effects and when the evaluation scope warrants it. For programme-level VfM evaluation (as opposed to area-based impact assessment), a multiplier of 1.0 is often appropriate.

displacement.note: Higher for retail/leisure (40-75%), lower for export-oriented manufacturing (10-15%)

leakage.note: Depends on area openness and commuting patterns

source.publication: Additionality Guide, 4th edition; The Appraisal Guide, 3rd edition
